exercises.explain: CycleGAN translates images by mapping textures, but it has trouble changing the underlying shape or geometry.

options.text: Changing texture while keeping the original shapes
Completely reshaping objects like turning cars into airplanes

exercises.prompt: What is CycleGAN best at doing?

cards.body: Imagine trying to paint a horse's stripes onto a zebra. Easy, right? But what if you were asked to paint a dog's floppy ears onto a cat? That's where CycleGAN runs into a wall!
cards.title: Texture vs. Shape
What's the Limit?
cards.body: CycleGAN is designed to translate style and texture between two domains, but it struggles with large geometric changes (modifying shapes) and can hallucinate patterns incorrectly onto the wrong surfaces.

cards.title: The Clay Sculptor
cards.body: Think of CycleGAN as a painter who only changes the colors and patterns of a sculpture, but never uses new clay. It can paint a clay horse like a zebra, but it cannot remodel a clay cat into a floppy-eared dog.

cards.title: The Cat-Dog Glitch
cards.body: If you feed a CycleGAN photos of cats to turn into dogs, it won't grow long dog ears. Instead, it might just paint creepy, dog-like eyes and fur textures directly onto the cat's stiff pointy-eared silhouette!

cards.title: Myth: Complete Morphing
cards.body: Many think CycleGAN can transform any object into any other. In reality, it cannot warp shapes or add new body structures; it only swaps surface-level styles.

right.text: Cat body attempting to get floppy dog ears. Fails!

left.text: Zebra stripes mapped onto horse body. Works perfectly!

options.text: It struggles to change the underlying geometry and shapes of objects.
It only works with black and white images.

exercises.prompt: Why does CycleGAN fail when trying to turn a cat into a dog?
exercises.explain: CycleGAN only alters surface patterns and textures, meaning it fails at tasks requiring physical structural changes, like reshaping ears or tails.

exercises.prompt: CycleGAN is great for style transfer but bad at altering the overall physical structure of objects.
exercises.explain: True. CycleGAN cannot warp geometry easily; it is built to translate texture and color while keeping the base shape intact.
When the shape difference is too large, CycleGAN fails because it cannot make major changes to the image's geometry.
exercises.sentence: CycleGAN struggles to translate images when there are large changes in [blank].
exercises.prompt: CycleGAN struggles to translate images when there are large changes in [blank].

exercises.explain: In the AI-generated translation, the pattern is incorrectly repeated or hallucinated onto objects that shouldn't have it, like zebra stripes appearing on the grass.
exercises.prompt: Which scenario shows a CycleGAN hallucination error?

options.text: A normal photo of a horse standing in an empty field.
An image of a horse where zebra stripes have bled onto the background grass.

exercises.prompt: What are the primary limitations of using CycleGAN?

options.text: Large geometric changes and pattern hallucination
Slow training speed and high memory usage only

exercises.explain: CycleGAN's main constraint is its inability to modify geometry and its tendency to hallucinate patterns where they don't belong. Next up: Progressive GAN & StyleGAN.

concept: CycleGAN struggles with large geometric changes (e.g. cat to dog) and can hallucinate patterns incorrectly. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Progressive GAN & StyleGAN.'
title: CycleGAN's Limitations
